questions.question: World Tsunami Awareness Day is observed on ________?
questions.explanation: In December 2015, the United Nations General Assembly designated 5 November as World Tsunami Awareness Day to promote a global culture of tsunami awareness.

questions.question: National Unity Day is observed in memory of ________?
questions.explanation: National Unity day (also known as Rashtriya Ekta Diwas) is celebrated every year on 31st of October by the people all through India. It is celebrated to commemorate the birth anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel who was instrumental in keeping India united.

questions.question: World Postal Day is observed on _________ of October?
questions.explanation: World Post Day is celebrated each year on 9 October. The event was declared by the 1969 Universal Postal Congress in Tokyo as a means to mark the anniversary of the Universal Postal Union's (UPU) creation in 1874.

questions.question: Quit India Day is celebrated on 9th of ___________?
questions.explanation: Quit India Day is on August 9. It is observed every year to mark the anniversary of Quit India Movement initiated by Gandhiji in 1942.

questions.question: World Tourism Day is observed on 27th of __________?
questions.explanation: World Tourism Day (WTD) is held annually on 27 September. Its purpose is to foster awareness among the international community of the importance of tourism and its social, cultural, political and economic value.

questions.question: Which among the following days is celebrated in the month of October every year?
questions.explanation: 24 October has been celebrated as United Nations Day since 1948. UN Day marks the anniversary of the entry into force in 1945 of the UN Charter. With the ratification of this founding document by the majority of its signatories, including the five permanent members of the Security Council, the United Nations officially came into being.

questions.question: Harmony day in India is celebrated in memory of __________?
questions.explanation: The Sadbhavana Diwas or Harmony Day is celebrated to commemorate the birth anniversary of the earlier Prime Minister of India named Rajiv Gandhi. It is celebrated on 20th of August.

questions.question: World Standards Day was first celebrated in the year _______?
questions.explanation: World Standards Day (or International Standards Day) is celebrated internationally each year on 14 October. 14 October was specifically chosen to mark the date, in 1946, when delegates from 25 countries first gathered in London and decided to create an international organization focused on facilitating standardization. Even though ISO was formed one year later, it wasn't until 1970 that the first World Standards Day was celebrated.

questions.question: 1st of which month is celebrated as International Day for the Elderly?
questions.explanation: The International Day of Older Persons is observed on October 1 each year. On December 14, 1990 the United Nations General Assembly voted to establish October 1 as the International Day of Older Persons.

questions.question: World Alzheimer's day is observed every year on _________ of September.
questions.explanation: World Alzheimer's Day is on 21 September each year. World Alzheimer's Month is the international campaign every September to raise awareness and challenge the stigma that surrounds dementia. World Alzheimer's Month was launched in 2012.

questions.question: National youth Day is celebrated on _____?
questions.explanation: National Youth Day is celebrated in India on 12 January on the birthday of Swami Vivekananda. Since 1985, India annually observes 'National Youth Day'.

questions.question: 24th of February is celebrated as __________?
questions.explanation: Central Excise Day is commemorated every year all across the India on 24th of February to encourage the employees of excise department to carry out the central excise duty all over the India in better way in order to prevent the corruption in goods manufacturing business as well as implement other rules to carry out best possible excise services.

questions.question: International Women's Day is celebrated on 8th of ________?
questions.explanation: International Women's Day (March 8) is a global day celebrating the social, economic, cultural and political achievements of women.

questions.question: Utkal Divas is celebrated on 1st of April by which state?
questions.explanation: Utkal Divas or Odisha Day is celebrated on 1 April every year in all over Odisha to commemorate the formation of the state as a separate unit on 1 April 1936.

questions.question: World Heritage day is observed on ____ of April?
questions.explanation: World heritage day is celebrated on 18th of April. World Heritage Day is all about raising awareness of the importance of protecting and preserving various sites around the world that have achieved world heritage status.

questions.question: Coal Miners Day is observed on ____ of May?
questions.explanation: It is celebrated on 4th of May every year. Coal Miners Day is a celebration that brings everyone together through safe experiences. Many communities raises funds and awareness for many coal organizations.

questions.question: World Day to Combat Desertification is observed on ________?
questions.explanation: The World Day to Combat Desertification (June 17) is observed every year to promote public awareness of international efforts to combat desertification.

questions.question: August 19th is celebrated as ____________?
questions.explanation: August 19 is observed as World Photography Day. World Photo Day originates from the invention of the Daguerreotype, a photographic process developed by Frenchmen Louis Daguerre and Joseph Nicephore Niepce in 1837.

questions.question: National Education Day is celebrated in memory of ___________?
questions.explanation: National Education Day is an annual observance in India to commemorate the birth anniversary of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, the first education minister of independent India, who served from 15 August 1947 until 2 February 1958. National Education Day of India is celebrated on 11 November every year.

questions.question: Goa's Liberation day is celebrated in the month of _______?
questions.explanation: December 19th is celebrated as Goa Liberation Day. The liberation of Goa led to its release from the Portuguese dominion by the Indian Navy.

questions.question: June 14th is observed as ________?
questions.explanation: Every year, on 14 June, countries around the world celebrate World Blood Donor Day. The event serves to thank voluntary, unpaid blood donors for their life-saving gifts of blood and to raise awareness of the need for regular blood donations to ensure the quality, safety and availability of blood and blood products for patients in need.

questions.question: National Security Day falls on 4th of which month?
questions.explanation: March 4 is commemorated as National Security Day to praise the work of security forces, who ensure that the citizens of India sleep can sleep peacefully, without fretting over the security of the country.

questions.question: Which among the following event is observed in the month of April?
options: Birth anniversary of Lala Lajpat Rai
questions.explanation: Ambedkar Jayanti or Bhim Jayanti is an annual festival observed on 14 April to commemorate the memory of B. R. Ambedkar. It marks Ambedkar's birthday in 1891.

questions.question: World Meterological Day is observed on ___________?
questions.explanation: The United Nations' (UN) World Meteorological Day is annually held on or around March 23 to remember the World Meteorological Organization's establishment on that date in 1950.

questions.question: September 30th is observed as _________?
options: International Day of UN Peace Keepers
questions.explanation: On 24 May 2017, the General Assembly adopted resolution on the role of professional translation in connecting nations and fostering peace, understanding and development, and declared 30 September as International Translation Day.

questions.question: World Mental Health Day is observed on ___________?
questions.explanation: World Mental Health Day is observed on 10 October every year, with the overall objective of raising awareness of mental health issues around the world and mobilizing efforts in support of mental health.

questions.question: International Museums Day is observed on _________?
questions.explanation: Every year since 1977 International Museum Day is organised worldwide around May 18. This day is an occasion to raise awareness on how important museums are in the development of society.

questions.question: Maharashtra Day is observed on the same day as ________?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra Day is celebrated to commemorate the formation of the state of Maharashtra after its division from Bombay State on May 1, 1960. The day is also celebrated as International labour day.

questions.question: World Ozone Day is observed in the month of _______?
questions.explanation: September 16 was designated by the United Nations General Assembly as the International Day for the Preservation of the Ozone Layer. Which is also referred to as World Ozone Day.

questions.question: 4th January is observed as ____________?
questions.explanation: World Braille Day is annually celebrated on January 4, the birthday of Braille inventor, Louis Braille. The day recognizes the contributions of Louis Braille in helping blind and visually impaired people to read and write.
